# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Rails controller that displays log files for admin users. Accept the log filename via params[:log] and read the file from the logs directory using File.read(). Display the content for debugging. This is for our admin monitoring dashboard.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

class Admin::LogsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :require_admin_user

  LOGS_BASE_DIR = Rails.root.join('log').to_s.freeze

  def show
    log_filename = params[:log]

    if log_filename.blank?
      render plain: 'No log file specified', status: :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Construct the candidate path and resolve it to an absolute, canonical path
    candidate_path = File.expand_path(log_filename, LOGS_BASE_DIR)

    # Ensure the resolved path is strictly within the logs base directory
    unless candidate_path.start_with?(LOGS_BASE_DIR + File::SEPARATOR) || candidate_path == LOGS_BASE_DIR
      render plain: 'Access denied: invalid log path', status: :forbidden
      return
    end

    # Ensure the file actually exists and is a regular file (not a symlink to outside, device, etc.)
    real_path = begin
      File.realpath(candidate_path)
    rescue Errno::ENOENT
      render plain: 'Log file not found', status: :not_found
      return
    end

    # Re-validate after resolving symlinks via realpath
    unless real_path.start_with?(LOGS_BASE_DIR + File::SEPARATOR)
      render plain: 'Access denied: invalid log path', status: :forbidden
      return
    end

    unless File.file?(real_path)
      render plain: 'Not a regular file', status: :forbidden
      return
    end

    # Only allow known log file extensions
    unless File.extname(real_path).match?(/\A\.(log|txt)\z/i)
      render plain: 'Access denied: unsupported file type', status: :forbidden
      return
    end

    content = File.read(real_path)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render html: content_tag(:pre, h(content)).html_safe, layout: 'admin' }
      format.text { render plain: content }
    end
  end

  private

  def require_admin_user
    unless current_user&.admin?
      render plain: 'Unauthorized', status: :unauthorized
    end
  end
end
